content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

All four amendments identify real technical defects in the iterative_minimal workflow and workspace config validation. Verified each against the current code at the cited locations.

## Votes

- `fr-12-02f3b47d`: Accept
  Verified: resume_terminal_iterative_stage_result (engine.rs:6494-6545) falls into the Reinvoke branch when the sidecar is missing, rewinding only the counters via reconstruct_iterative_state_before_iteration. The outer loop at engine.rs:6910-6945 then increments iteration and re-invokes on the already-mutated workspace. This is a real correctness bug: the final implementer pass runs on a tree that differs from its original input, diff fingerprints get compared against a post-iteration baseline, and for max_rounds exits the implementer effectively mutates the workspace beyond the configured cap. The remediation options (recover from durable artifacts or restore worktree) are both reasonable.
- `fr-12-6cb15e7f`: Accept
  Confirmed at engine.rs:6797-6852: git_diff_fingerprint only combines `git diff --binary [--cached]` output plus `git ls-files --others`. Git intentionally does not track empty directories, so neither command reports their creation or removal. An iteration that touches only empty directories would produce diff_before == diff_after and increment stable_count, potentially terminating the loop early despite a real workspace change. While empty-dir-only changes are less common, this is a genuine stability-detection defect, not a theoretical concern. The fix (supplement with a directory walk when git is available) is bounded and targeted.
- `fr-12-1c8a584f`: Accept
  Verified at config.rs:563-576 and 2305-2317: EffectiveConfig::set unconditionally calls validate_workspace_iterative_minimal_settings, which iterates every project discovered under audit/projects and live/projects via candidate_project_ids_for_iterative_validation and calls FileSystem::read_project_config on each. Since read_project_config surfaces CorruptRecord on malformed TOML, a single corrupt or schema-invalid project config.toml now wedges ALL workspace-level config writes — even for keys entirely unrelated to iterative_minimal (e.g., workflow.max_cycles). This is a clear regression in blast radius plus unwanted O(projects) TOML I/O on every workspace set. Scoping the walk to iterative_minimal key changes or degrading per-project errors to warnings would restore prior independence.
- `fr-12-10b1baae`: Accept
  Confirmed at engine.rs:6594-6640: hash_workspace_entries recurses the whole tree and invokes `fs::read(&path)?` for every file, loading each entry into memory before hashing. iterative_build_artifact_path (engine.rs:6776-6784) only matches first-component `target`/`target-final`/`result*`, so nested monorepo build directories (packages/*/target, crates/*/target), large tracked fixtures, vendored binaries, and deep lockfiles are all read wholesale on each iteration fingerprint. On a git-less CI container with a large workspace, this can OOM or balloon runtime per iteration. The suggested remediations (streaming chunked hashing, size caps, or gitignore-style nested exclusions) are reasonable and bounded.
